rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL MURDOCH et al VS. BROCK SOLUTIONS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 6, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT ELECTRICAL SYSTEMS & INSTALLATIONS, INC'S (ESI) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The Court grants summary judgment. The undisputed evidence shows that defendant did not owe plaintiff a duty of care at the time of the accident. The evidence shows that defendant did not start the baggage conveyor, was not on the floor when the injury occurred, defendant was merely working on drawings when it returned to the worksite on January 10, 2011, defendant did not supervise or control plaintiff's work, and plaintiff never reported to defendant. (UMF 16-22). In opposition, plaintiff fails to create a triable issue of material fact. Plaintiff argues that defendant and Bass were joint venturers on the project and defendant is vicariously liable for the negligence of Mr. Zuniga, Bass' employee. The complaint does not raise a joint venture theory of liability, see Simmons v. Ware (2013) 213 Cal.App.4th 1035, 1049, and plaintiff cannot avoid summary judgment by relying on unpleaded claims, see Hutton v. Fidelity National Title Co. (2013) 213 Cal.App.4th 486, 493. The Court rejects defendant's primary assumption of the risk argument. Defendant's authorities are factually distinguishable and no case holds that a worker on a construction site assumes all the risks present at that site. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: MARITZA HURTADO VS. THE KROGER CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Cala Foods, Inc., Dba Foods Co., To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And For The Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, March 6, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT CALA FOODS, INC. DBA FOODS CO.'s Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And For The Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff. Pro Tem Judge Peter VanZandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Cala Foods, Inc. dba Foods Co.'s motion to Compel Responses to Special Interrogatories is GRANTED. Defendant has established that plaintiff Maritza Hurtado was aware of the Special Interrogatories, promised responses on a 10 day extension offered by Defendant and failed to provide responses within the 10 day extension or anytime thereafter. Plaintiff is hereby ORDERED to provide verified responses without objections to Defendant within 20 days of service of notice of entry of the order on this motion. Defendant's request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. Plaintiff is warned that failure to comply with this Order may subject her to terminating sanctions upon application by Defendant. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com or a fax to 415.391.8766 with a copy to all other parties by 4pm one day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL VS. BANK OF THE WEST, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Bnak Of The West'S Further Responses To Ptlf'S Fifth Request For Production Of Documents, And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions Against Deft And Its Counsel Of $1602.50
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, March 6, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL's Motion To Compel Bank Of The West'S Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Fifth Request For Production Of Documents, And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Its Counsel Of $1602.50. Pro Tem Judge Peter VanZandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Bank of The West's Further Responses to Plaintiff's Fifth Request for Production of Documents and for an Award of Monetary sanctions is DENIED without prejudice. The Court finds that there was an inadequate effort by Plaintiff's counsel to meet and confer in advance of bringing this motion as required. (CCP Section 2016.040) Plaintiff's claims of prior efforts to meet and confer on the ESI data are insufficient to establish a meet and confer on this motion. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com or a fax to 415.391.8766 with a copy to all other parties by 4pm one day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL VS. BANK OF THE WEST, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Deposition Testimony Of John Thomason And Award Of Monetary Sanctionsa Gainst Deft And Its Counsel Littler Medelson In The Amount Of $1915.0
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, March 6, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL's Motion To Compel Further Deposition Testimony Of John Thomason And Award Of Monetary Sanctions against Defendant And Its Counsel Littler Medelson In The Amount Of $1915.00. Pro Tem Judge Peter VanZandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Further Deposition Testimony of John Thomason and for an Award of Monetary sanctions is DENIED. Plaintiff has failed to overcome the presumption of confidentiality of the communications between John Thomason and in-house counsel. Defendant has adequately shown that the dominant purpose of the communications between in-house counsel and Mr. Thomason and other executives of Bank of the West related to Plaintiff was lawyer-client. (Clark v. Superior Court (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 37, 51). Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com or a fax to 415.391.8766 with a copy to all other parties by 4pm one day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CITY CENTRAL OF SAN FRANCISCO HOMEOWNERS VS. AJIT SINGH BHALLA, AN INDIVIDUAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 6, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT ELIZABETH KOWAL's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons; Denied. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted. The evidence establishes that Defendant Elizabeth Kowal purposefully availed herself of the forum by purchasing property in California in 2004. (Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice ("RJN"), Ex. A.) Mrs. Kowal also is listed on a Deed of Trust for the property, which indicates that she had contracted to make monthly payments to a lender located in California for a 30 year period. (RJN, Ex. C.) The evidence further establishes that the suit is related to Mrs. Kowal's activities in California, as the complaint seeks recovery of Homeowner's Association assessments due by virtue of her ownership of the property. Therefore, Plaintiff has established that there are requisite minimum contacts supporting specific personal jurisdiction, which shifts the burden to plaintiff to show that jurisdiction is not reasonable. (Integral Development Corp. v. Weissenbach (2002) 99 Cal.App.4th 576, 591.) Plaintiff has not met her burden. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO, INC.,A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. MICHAEL S KENNEDY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 6, 2014, Line 15, PLAINTIFF SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION's MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT Granted, in part. Attachment in the amount of $ 121,162.03 shall issue against Defendant Bay View Rehabilitation Hospital, LLC. Before the issuance of the writ of attachment, Plaintiff Sysco San Francisco, Inc. shall file an undertaking of $ 10,000.00. (CCP §§ 489.210, 489.220.) Plaintiff has established the probable validity of its claim against Defendant and that the sum is a fixed and ascertainable amount of $ 121,162.03. (Ableman Decl., 4, Exhibit A.) No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required provide proof of filing an undertaking in the amount of $ 10,000.00 and to prepare a proposed order on the Judicial Council form with the name and address of the Plaintiff's counsel. =302/eg

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 6, 2014, Line 18, Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction Continued to March 13, 2014 to give Defendant the opportunity to serve Plaintiff with his opposition papers.
